mappings.rationale: Dance in the theatre is explicitly addressed in section A.5; theatre covers performance, staging, and the integration of dance with dramatic arts.
Ethnomusicology encompasses dance in non-Western cultures and its relation to music, directly relevant to primitive, folk, and popular dance (D).
Covers aesthetic theory including performance and embodied experience, relevant to the nature of dance as art and dance criticism (A.1, A.4).
Covers ritual, rites of passage, and ceremonial practices, directly relevant to the origins and functions of dance and primitive dance forms (A.1, D).
Covers ethnographic study of cultural practices including dance, relevant to primitive, folk, and popular dance (D).
Folk music is inseparably tied to folk dance traditions across cultures, relevant to folk and popular dance (D).
Modern drama incorporates dance and movement, relevant to the relation of modern dance to drama and musical theatre (C.2).
Music is integral to ballet, modern dance, and theatrical dance; covers performance and production relevant to the integration of dance with music (A.5, B.2).
Dance in motion pictures is explicitly mentioned in section A.5, and modern dance's relation to film is addressed in C.2.
Covers creative expression and artistic process, relevant to choreography and the nature of dance as an expressive art (A.1, A.2).
